title: Marbella Hen Do: Private Boat Hire Guide and Costs 2026
html:
For a hen do with a difference, skippered charters in Marbella offer a sophisticated way to celebrate along the Costa del Sol. Departing from Puerto Banús, private boat hire for groups starts around €1,500 for a half-day excursion, and BoatHire24 connects you directly with verified boat owners for a seamless booking.

Where to set off in Marbella

The best departure points for a hen do boat trip are Puerto Banús, Marbella Marina (Puerto Deportivo de Marbella), and Cabopino Marina. Puerto Banús is the glamorous hub with designer boutiques and restaurants, while Marbella Marina offers a more relaxed vibe close to the old town. Cabopino, a smaller marina to the east, gives quick access to the sand dunes of Artola. Further along the coast, you can anchor off the bays of Calahonda or even cruise to the chic port of Sotogrande for a lunch stop.

Which boat to rent

  1. Motor Yacht (Azimut or Sunseeker): Ideal for a stylish party on the water, accommodating up to twelve guests. A half-day charter from €2,000.
  2. Catamaran (Lagoon): Spacious and stable, perfect for larger hens wanting sunbathing space. Sleeps ten comfortably; from €2,500 for a full day.
  3. RIB (Axopar): Fast and sporty, great for a short, exhilarating cruise with water toys. Designed for eight guests; from €900 for four hours.
  4. Sailing Yacht (Bavaria): Classic and elegant, suitable for a more relaxed sail with cocktails. Accommodates six; from €1,200 for a half day.
  5. Luxury Flybridge (Fairline): Combines indoor and outdoor living with a crew. Up to ten guests; from €3,000 for a full day.

How much does it cost to rent a boat in Marbella for a hen do?

Prices vary by boat type and duration, but a typical private hen do charter ranges from €900 to €3,500.

  • Shared sunset tour: €60, €90 per person, minimum group of 8
  • Small motorboat (half day, 4 hours): from €900 for up to 8 guests
  • Catamaran (full day, 8 hours): from €2,500 for up to 12 guests
  • Crewed luxury yacht (full day with skipper and host): from €3,500 for up to 12 guests

When comparing options, many groups find that booking through a dedicated platform like skippered charters in Marbella provides value and peace of mind, as all listings are verified and include crew.

Do you need a licence to rent a boat in Marbella?

For a private hen do with a skipper, no licence is required; the operator provides a certified captain. Spanish regulations require a licence for bareboat charters (usually a PPER or ICC), but most hen do groups prefer a crewed charter so everyone can relax. The operator confirms exact requirements at booking.

Local tip: In July and August, the Levanter wind can create choppy seas in the afternoon, so plan your hen do for a morning departure from Puerto Banús for calmer waters and better photo light.

At a glance

  • Price band: €900, €3,500 for private hire
  • Peak months: June, July, August, September
  • Trip length: 4 hours (half day) or 8 hours (full day)
  • Capacity: up to 12 guests per boat
  • Currency: EUR

Questions about boat rental in Marbella

Can we bring our own food and drink on board?

Yes, most charter boats allow you to bring your own catering, though some skippers charge a corkage fee of around €5 per bottle.

What happens if the weather is bad on our booked day?

The skipper will assess conditions and may postpone the trip at no extra cost or offer a full refund if it is unsafe to sail.

How far in advance should we book for a hen do?

For peak summer weekends, book at least two to three months ahead to secure your preferred boat and time slot.

Is it customary to tip the crew in Marbella?

Yes, a tip of 10, 15% of the charter fee is appreciated and shared among the skipper and host.

For the perfect hen do, one popular choice is a 50-foot Prestige 500, which offers plenty of deck space and a flybridge for dancing. You can compare features on the Prestige 500 charter page to see if it suits your group size and budget. In 2026, early booking is recommended given rising demand for private experiences on the coast.

Whether you choose a sleek Azimut or a spacious Lagoon catamaran, starting from Puerto Banús ensures a memorable celebration. If you own a boat in Marbella, consider listing it on the BoatHire24 marketplace to reach hens worldwide. Written by the BoatHire24 team, who book and list verified boats in Marbella and hundreds of destinations worldwide.
